jobs.purpose: Per health check per interval. Dispatched by the HealthCheckObserver.created path + rescheduled by itself for the next interval. Executes the check via the appropriate HealthCheckExecutor strategy (db_ping / redis_ping / http_endpoint / …) + persists a health_check_runs row.
steps: Load health_checks row; refuse when is_enabled = false OR deleted_at IS NOT NULL
Refuse when the tenant no longer holds monitoring_capture entitlement (logs warning; disables the check)
Resolve HealthCheckExecutor strategy for check.check_type
Execute the probe with check.timeout_seconds abort threshold; record start + end time
Scan response_body for PII regex matches; redact detected patterns
Truncate response_body to 8KB max
Insert health_check_runs row with status + latency_ms + response_body + error_details
Fire HealthCheckRun event
Reschedule self at now() + check.interval_seconds
jobs.note: tries=1 because the retry semantics for health checks are handled by the next scheduled run — a transient timeout is captured as a status='timeout' run, not retried.

jobs.purpose: Every 30s sweep. Scans monitoring_alert_policies for each tenant + evaluates thresholds against the trailing window_seconds of health_check_runs (for health_check_status signals) OR pulled metrics (for error_rate / latency_p95 / queue_depth / worker_health / custom_metric signals).
steps: SELECT id FROM monitoring_alert_policies WHERE is_enabled = true AND (suppression_config->>'silenced_until')::timestamptz IS NULL OR (suppression_config->>'silenced_until')::timestamptz < now() FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED LIMIT 1000
For each policy: check the after_hours_suppress + weekend_suppress flags against the tenant's timezone; skip when suppressed
Compute the aggregate signal over the trailing window_seconds via AlertPolicyEvaluator
Compare against threshold_operator + threshold_value
When threshold breached: increment consecutive_breach_count; when count >= consecutive_breaches_required: fire FireAlertJob(policy_id, signal_value, signal_context)
When threshold no longer breached AND an alert is currently firing for this policy: dispatch MonitoringAlertObserver.autoResolve via MonitoringAlertRepository::autoResolveForPolicy()

jobs.purpose: Fired by EvaluateAlertPoliciesJob when a threshold breach is confirmed. Inserts the monitoring_alerts row + fires MonitoringAlertFired.
steps: Load monitoring_alert_policies row; refuse when is_enabled = false (someone silenced between eval and fire)
Re-check suppression_config (idempotency guard); when suppressed: insert alert with status='suppressed' + fire MonitoringAlertSuppressed + return
Insert monitoring_alerts row with status='firing' + severity from policy + signal_value + signal_context
Fire MonitoringAlertFired (this triggers the GroupAlertsIntoIncidentJob + NotifyProvidersJob fan-out)
jobs.note: NOT unique — multiple fires for the same policy within the window are expected when the breach is sustained. Deduplication happens at the incident-grouping layer.

jobs.purpose: Fired on MonitoringAlertFired. Attaches the alert to an existing incident OR opens a new one.
steps: Load monitoring_alerts row

SELECT id FROM monitoring_incidents WHERE tenant_id = ? AND status IN ('open', 'acknowledged', 'investigating', 'mitigating') AND severity = ? AND opened_at > now() - interval '5 minutes' AND (affected_services @> to_jsonb(?) OR affected_services @> '[]')
When found: update alert.monitoring_incident_id + increment incident.alert_count; return
When not found: insert monitoring_incidents row with status='open' + affected_services from alert.signal_context + oncall_page_config from policy + fire MonitoringIncidentOpened

jobs.purpose: Delayed job. Fires when the incident's SLA elapses without acknowledgment. Pages the escalation contacts per oncall_page_config.escalation_policy.
steps: Load monitoring_incidents row; refuse when status != 'open' (someone ack'd or resolved between schedule and fire)
Refuse when acknowledged_at IS NOT NULL
Fire MonitoringIncidentEscalatedNotification (pages backup on-call via oncall_page_config.escalation_policy)
Dispatch NotifyProvidersJob for the escalation targets

jobs.purpose: Nightly. Sweeps monitoring_incidents where status IN ('open', 'acknowledged') AND every attached alert has status IN ('resolved', 'suppressed', 'expired'). Auto-transitions the incident to 'resolved'.
steps: SELECT i.id FROM monitoring_incidents i WHERE i.status IN ('open', 'acknowledged') AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM monitoring_alerts a WHERE a.monitoring_incident_id = i.id AND a.status = 'firing') AND i.opened_at < now() - interval '1 hour' LIMIT 500
For each: transition incident.status = 'resolved' + resolved_at = now() + resolved_by = 'auto' + fire MonitoringIncidentResolved

jobs.purpose: Fan-out job. Loads the policy's notify_provider_configs (or the incident's oncall_page_config), evaluates severity routing via MonitoringPagingCoordinator, dispatches one HTTP call per eligible provider.
steps: Load monitoring_alert OR monitoring_incident (payload discriminant)
Resolve targets via MonitoringPagingCoordinator::providersForSeverity($severity, $notify_provider_configs)
For each provider_config: check CircuitBreakerGate::allow($tenant, $provider); when open: fire MonitoringProviderDispatchSuppressedByBreaker + skip
Transform payload via MonitoringPayloadTransformer::transformFor($provider, $alert_or_incident, $config)
Dispatch via Guzzle with per-provider timeout (default 15s, config.timeout_seconds override)
On 2xx + validation pass: increment last_success_at + reset consecutive_failure_count = 0
On failure: increment consecutive_failure_count + set last_failure_at; when count >= failure_threshold: fire OpenCircuitBreakerJob(tenant_id, provider); schedule retry via RetryFailedNotificationsJob
jobs.note: tries=1 because retry semantics are OWNED by this module via RetryFailedNotificationsJob + backoff schedule. Do NOT enable Laravel-level retry — it would bypass the circuit-breaker gate.

jobs.purpose: Every 5 min sweep. Picks up failed provider notifications (transient failures) + re-dispatches via NotifyProvidersJob with attempt_number+1.
steps: SELECT id FROM monitoring_notification_attempts WHERE status='failed' AND is_transient=true AND next_retry_at <= now() AND attempt_number < max_attempts FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED LIMIT 500
For each: enqueue NotifyProvidersJob with attempt_number + 1
Clear next_retry_at to avoid double-fire before the new attempt runs

jobs.purpose: Nightly. Applies the health_check_runs retention window: 7-day hot / 30-day cold default; 30-day hot / 90-day cold for Enterprise tenants with monitoring_extended_retention.
steps: For each tenant: determine retention window from monitoring_extended_retention entitlement
DELETE FROM health_check_runs WHERE tenant_id = ? AND created_at < now() - (?::int * interval '1 day') LIMIT 10000
Emit metric stackra.monitoring.retention.health_check_runs_purged_total

jobs.purpose: Weekly. Applies the monitoring_alerts + monitoring_incidents retention window: 30-day hot / 90-day cold default; 90-day hot / 1-year cold for Enterprise; indefinite retention on incidents for Enterprise with monitoring_extended_retention.
steps: For each tenant: determine retention windows from monitoring_extended_retention entitlement
DELETE FROM monitoring_alerts WHERE tenant_id = ? AND status IN ('resolved', 'expired', 'suppressed') AND created_at < now() - (?::int * interval '1 day') LIMIT 10000
DELETE FROM monitoring_incidents WHERE tenant_id = ? AND status = 'resolved' AND resolved_at < now() - (?::int * interval '1 day') AND NOT (?::boolean AND severity = 'p1') LIMIT 5000
Note: P1 incidents on Enterprise-with-extended-retention tenants are NEVER auto-purged (postmortem archive)
Emit metrics stackra.monitoring.retention.alerts_purged_total + stackra.monitoring.retention.incidents_purged_total
